tiktok.caption: You maxed your 401k early — and your employer stopped matching. 43% of plans have no true-up. Here's the $4,300 trap.

youtube_short.title: Maxing Your 401k Too Fast Costs You $4,300
title_alternatives: The 401k Trap Every Diligent Saver Falls Into
Your Employer Stopped Matching — Here's Why
youtube_short.description: Maxing your 401k as fast as possible sounds smart — but if your employer matches per paycheck and your plan doesn't have a true-up provision, you lose the match for every pay period after you hit the limit. 43% of plans have no true-up. Max out by October and you forfeit 12 paychecks of free money — that's $4,300 gone. The fix is simple: divide $23,500 by your pay periods and contribute evenly all year. Same max, full match, every time. #401k #personalfinance #retirementplanning — Follow for the money traps nobody warns you about.

hook_line: You maxed your 401k early. Your employer stopped matching.
